title: Cyclosorus holophyllus
taxonomy: Cyclosorus holophyllus is classified within the family Thelypteridaceae, which is a group of ferns. This plant is further classified within the genus Cyclosorus, which is a type of fern. The genus Cyclosorus is part of the family Thelypteridaceae. The Thelypteridaceae family is a group of ferns that are commonly known as the marsh ferns or the thelypterid ferns. Cyclosorus holophyllus is also classified within the division Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns.

intro: Cyclosorus holophyllus, also known as the Chinese ladder fern or ladder spleenwort, is a species of fern native to eastern Asia. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and inflammation, due to its potential to lower body temperature and alleviate respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Cyclosorus holophyllus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Cyclosorus holophyllus is commonly prepared as a tea or decoction, and is also used in traditional Chinese medicine to make tablets, capsules, and other formulations.
